calls.content:
### Work Breakdown Structure

- **Site Preparation (10 crew days)**  
  - Clear and level the site (3 crew days)  
  - Install temporary fencing and signage (2 crew days)  
  - Set up site office and storage (3 crew days)  
  - Conduct soil testing and compaction (2 crew days)  

- **Foundation Work (25 crew days)**  
  - Excavate for footings (5 crew days)  
  - Form and pour footings (10 crew days)  
  - Install foundation waterproofing (3 crew days)  
  - Backfill and compact around foundation (5 crew days)  
  - Inspect and approve foundation (2 crew days)  

- **Structural Framing (30 crew days)**  
  - Erect steel or wood framing (15 crew days)  
  - Install roof trusses and sheathing (8 crew days)  
  - Install exterior sheathing and bracing (5 crew days)  
  - Inspect structural integrity (2 crew days)  

- **Roofing and Exterior (20 crew days)**  
  - Install roofing materials (8 crew days)  
  - Install windows and exterior doors (6 crew days)  
  - Apply exterior finishes (siding, stucco, etc.) (6 crew days)  

- **Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing (MEP) (40 crew days)**  
  - Rough-in electrical wiring and panels (12 crew days)  
  - Rough-in plumbing lines and fixtures (10 crew days)  
  - Install HVAC systems (10 crew days)  
  - Inspect and approve MEP rough-ins (8 crew days)  

- **Interior Finishes (35 crew days)**  
  - Install insulation and vapor barriers (7 crew days)  
  - Hang and finish drywall (12 crew days)  
  - Paint interior walls and ceilings (8 crew days)  
  - Install flooring (carpet, tile, etc.) (6 crew days)  
  - Install interior doors and trim (2 crew days)  

- **Final Touches and Cleanup (15 crew days)**  
  - Install shelving, counters, and fixtures (8 crew days)  
  - Final cleaning and debris removal (5 crew days)  
  - Conduct final inspections and obtain approvals (2 crew days)  

### Total Estimate  
175 crew days  

### Assumptions  
- Standard urban site conditions with no unexpected soil or utility issues.  
- All materials are delivered on time and in full.  
- Weather conditions do not cause significant delays.  
- Subcontractors are available and work in parallel where possible.  
- No major design changes occur during construction.  
- Inspections are completed within standard timelines.  
- Crew size is consistent throughout the project.
